_comment: Edit the values below. Do not change the keys (left of the colon). Keys are used to find where text appears on the site.

home.meta_description: Professional support for Portugal residency pathways including D2, D7, and D8 visas. Secure document handling, guided preparation, and application tracking.
home.hero_subtitle: Clear, structured support for D2, D7, and D8 visa applications. We help you organise documents, understand requirements, and move forward with confidence.
home.why_teaser_text: Lifestyle, stability, and access to Europe. See why Portugal remains a leading destination for international relocation.
home.services_subtitle: Practical immigration support for individuals and families planning a move to Portugal.
home.service_d2_desc: For founders, investors, and business owners building or expanding commercial activity in Portugal.
home.service_d7_desc: For retirees and applicants with regular income from pensions, investments, property, or similar sources.
home.service_d8_desc: For remote employees and freelancers working for employers or clients based outside Portugal.
home.who_subtitle: Different visa routes fit different goals. We help you choose the most suitable path and prepare properly.
home.who_investors_desc: Applicants planning to establish, develop, or relocate business activity in Portugal.
home.who_nomads_desc: Employees, consultants, and freelancers working online while living legally in Portugal.
home.who_retirees_desc: Applicants relying on passive income and families planning a long-term relocation.
home.why_us_subtitle: A professional, organised approach to Portuguese residency preparation.
home.why_us_direct_desc: A single point of contact and a clear process from onboarding through submission support.
home.why_us_expert_desc: Guidance built around active Portuguese immigration procedures and practical document preparation.
home.why_us_communication_desc: Straightforward communication in English and Portuguese so you always know what comes next.
home.why_us_accountability_desc: Support across eligibility review, document organisation, application preparation, and follow-up.
home.why_us_confidentiality_desc: Sensitive documents and personal information are handled with strong security controls.
home.why_us_dashboard_desc: Upload documents, track progress, and stay in touch with your specialist in one place.
home.licensed_subtitle: We work with qualified professionals and apply secure standards to client data and documentation.
home.final_cta_title: Start Planning Your Move to Portugal
home.final_cta_lead: Request an eligibility review and we will assess whether your profile appears suitable for the D2, D7, or D8 pathway.
home.footer_tagline: Independent immigration support for Portugal residency pathways including D2, D7, and D8, with guided preparation, document management, and progress tracking.
home.footer_legal: Terms of Service · Privacy Policy · Cookies · © {year} WINIT. All rights reserved. WINIT is an independent immigration support service and is not affiliated with the Portuguese government, AIMA, any embassy, or any consulate. Immigration outcomes depend on government authorities. Our services provide guidance, document preparation assistance, and process support only. Visa approval cannot be guaranteed.

contact.hero_subtitle: Request an eligibility review or contact our team to discuss your residency goals, timeline, and preferred visa route.
contact.info_lead: Choose the contact method that suits you best. We usually respond within one business day.
contact.eligibility_text: We review your circumstances to see whether the D2, D7, or D8 pathway may be appropriate and whether we can offer service support for your case.
contact.form_lead: Complete the form below and our team will respond with next steps.
contact.success_text: We will reply by email as soon as possible with guidance on the next stage of your enquiry.
contact.placeholder_message: Example: I am exploring the D7 route and would like an eligibility review.

faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
faq.q1: Is this service legitimate and secure?
faq.a1: Yes. We provide independent immigration support under written service terms. We may work alongside qualified professionals where needed, and client documents are handled using secure systems and restricted access procedures.
faq.q2: How long does the visa process take?
faq.a2: Processing times vary by visa category, consular workload, document readiness, and government review. Many applicants should expect the overall process to take several months, but timelines are never guaranteed.
faq.q3: What if I am not sure which visa I need?
faq.a3: Start with an eligibility review. We assess your income source, work model, business plans, and relocation goals to help identify the most suitable route.
faq.a4: Many Portuguese residency categories support family reunification or dependent inclusion, subject to government rules and financial thresholds. We explain the documentation required for each family member.
faq.a5: Creating an account does not require payment. If your matter is a fit for our services, package options, fees, and payment terms are discussed before formal engagement.

services.hero_subtitle: Professional immigration support for international clients relocating to Portugal. We help with residency planning, document preparation, and process coordination for D2, D7, and D8 applications.
services.section_subtitle: Choose the visa route that best matches your financial profile, work model, and relocation plans.
services.d2_desc: A business-focused residency route for entrepreneurs, founders, and investors launching or expanding operations in Portugal.
services.d7_desc: A residency route for applicants with stable recurring income, such as pensions, dividends, rentals, or investment returns.
services.d8_desc: A remote-work residency route for professionals earning income from employers or clients located outside Portugal.
services.helper_text: Not sure which route fits your profile?
services.cta_heading: Need Help Choosing the Right Visa?
services.cta_subtitle: Request an eligibility review and we will explain which route may best fit your circumstances and documents.

how_we_work.meta_title: Our Process — WINIT Portugal Residency Support
how_we_work.hero_subtitle: A structured process for choosing the right route, organising documents, receiving review, and preparing for the next stage of your residency journey.
how_we_work.hero_tagline: Clear guidance from first enquiry to submission support.
how_we_work.bridge_text: Four stages · One dashboard · One coordinated process
how_we_work.steps_subtitle: Transparent, organised, and designed to reduce uncertainty.
how_we_work.step1_desc: Choose your intended route and share your basic profile. Once we confirm fit, we explain service scope, fees, and next steps.
how_we_work.step2_desc: Use your checklist to upload supporting documents and keep everything organised in one secure space.
how_we_work.step3_desc: Your documents are reviewed for completeness, consistency, and next-stage readiness. We flag missing items and practical issues.
how_we_work.step4_desc: We guide you through the preparation phase and support the steps that follow, including the residency process after approval where applicable.
how_we_work.benefits_intro: We designed the workflow to keep your application organised, transparent, and easier to manage.
how_we_work.benefit1_text: A clear checklist and practical guidance
how_we_work.benefit2_text: Track progress and next actions in real time
how_we_work.benefit3_text: Direct access to your assigned specialist
how_we_work.cta_lead: Create an account for an eligibility review or contact us to discuss your case and likely next steps.

why_portugal.meta_title: Why Portugal — Residency, Lifestyle, and Opportunity | WINIT
why_portugal.heading: Why Portugal?
why_portugal.subtitle: A leading European destination for residency, lifestyle, and long-term planning
why_portugal.lifestyle_body: Portugal attracts international residents with its climate, coastline, cultural heritage, and slower pace of daily life. For many applicants, it offers a practical balance between comfort, accessibility, and long-term stability.
why_portugal.safety_title: A Stable Place to Build a Future
why_portugal.safety_body: Portugal is widely recognised for personal safety, social stability, and livability. These factors make it especially attractive to families, retirees, and professionals planning a long-term move.
why_portugal.digital_title: A Strong Base for Modern Work
why_portugal.digital_body: From remote work to entrepreneurship, Portugal continues to attract globally mobile professionals. Reliable infrastructure, international communities, and growing business ecosystems support both lifestyle and productivity.
why_portugal.value_body: Compared with many major Western European cities, Portugal can offer a high quality of life with comparatively moderate living costs, making it appealing for applicants planning both relocation and financial sustainability.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.section1_body: We collect information you provide through account registration, profile forms, document uploads, payment-related interactions, and direct communication with our team. This may include identification details, contact information, nationality, and application-support documents.
privacy.section2_body: We use your information to manage your account, provide immigration support services, communicate with you, coordinate document preparation, maintain internal records, and meet legal or regulatory obligations. We do not sell your personal data.
privacy.section3_body: We apply technical and organisational safeguards, including encryption, controlled access, and secure storage practices, to protect your information. Access is limited to authorised personnel and service providers involved in supporting your matter.
privacy.section4_body: You may request access to, correction of, or deletion of your personal data, subject to applicable law. Where GDPR applies, you may also have rights relating to restriction, objection, portability, and complaint to a supervisory authority.
privacy.section5_body: We use essential cookies required for site functionality and may use optional cookies if you consent through our cookie settings. Please review our Cookie Policy for more detail.
privacy.placeholder_note: Additional privacy terms should be reviewed and finalised with qualified counsel for your business, jurisdiction, and data flows.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.section1_body: By accessing or using WINIT, you agree to these terms and any policies referenced within them. If you do not agree, please do not use the service.
terms.section2_body: WINIT provides independent immigration guidance, document preparation assistance, account management tools, and related administrative support for Portugal residency matters. WINIT is not a government authority and is not affiliated with the Portuguese government, AIMA, any embassy, or any consulate.
terms.section3_body: You are responsible for providing complete, truthful, and up-to-date information and documents. You remain responsible for reviewing your application materials and for complying with all official rules, deadlines, and evidence requirements.
terms.section4_body: Service fees, payment terms, and any refund conditions are communicated separately during onboarding or engagement. Government fees, consular charges, translation costs, courier fees, and third-party expenses are separate unless stated otherwise in writing.
terms.section5_body: Immigration outcomes depend on government authorities. Our services provide guidance, document preparation assistance, and process support only. Visa approval cannot be guaranteed. Information on this website is for general guidance and does not constitute legal, tax, or financial advice. To the maximum extent permitted by law, liability is limited as set out in the applicable service agreement.
terms.placeholder_note: These terms should be reviewed and tailored by qualified counsel before production use.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.section1_body: Cookies are small data files stored on your device when you visit a website. They help the site function properly and can also remember settings or support analytics where permitted.
cookies.section2_body: We use essential cookies to operate the site, including account login and basic platform functionality. With your consent, we may also use optional cookies for analytics and performance improvement. We do not use cookies for behavioural advertising.
cookies.section3_body: You can manage cookie preferences through our cookie settings or your browser controls. Disabling essential cookies may affect core site functionality.

visa_d2.meta_title: Portugal Entrepreneur Visa (D2) 2026 — Business Residency Support | WINIT
visa_d2.meta_description: Explore the Portugal D2 Entrepreneur Visa for founders, investors, and business owners. Learn the requirements, process, and practical support available for business-based residency.
visa_d2.hero_title: Portugal D2 Entrepreneur Visa 2026 — Residency Through Business Activity
visa_d2.hero_subtitle: The D2 route is designed for non-EU applicants planning to establish, expand, or operate a business in Portugal. It can be suitable for founders, consultants, investors, and business owners seeking residency through genuine commercial activity.
visa_d2.overview_heading: What is the D2 Entrepreneur Visa?
visa_d2.overview_p1: The D2 visa is a Portuguese residency route for non-EU citizens who intend to carry out entrepreneurial or business activity in Portugal. Rather than focusing on a fixed investment threshold, authorities generally assess the credibility of the business, its practical viability, and the applicant's ability to support implementation.
visa_d2.overview_p2: Startup founders, business owners opening a Portuguese company or branch, consultants establishing local operations, and investors actively involved in a Portuguese commercial project.
visa_d2.investment_key_text: There is usually no single mandatory investment amount written into the route. What matters is whether the proposed business appears credible, adequately funded, and realistic in the Portuguese market context.
visa_d2.inv_item1_text: The amount required depends on the nature of the business, expected setup costs, and operational needs. Applications should show that the project is realistically financed.
visa_d2.inv_item2_text: Applicants should also demonstrate that they can support themselves and any dependants during the initial stages of the move and business setup.
visa_d2.inv_item3_text: A well-prepared business plan is central to the application. It should explain the model, market, expected revenue, financial assumptions, and broader economic rationale.
visa_d2.benefits_heading: Main Advantages of the D2 Route
visa_d2.benefit1: Business-led residency — Build your project in Portugal while pursuing lawful residence.
visa_d2.benefit2: No rigid investment threshold — The route focuses on viability and preparation rather than a single fixed amount.
visa_d2.benefit3: Operational flexibility — Suitable for a range of legitimate business activities and sectors.
visa_d2.benefit4: Family options — Eligible family members may be included or joined later under applicable rules.
visa_d2.benefit5: Schengen mobility — Residents may travel within the Schengen area subject to applicable limits.
visa_d2.benefit6: Long-term pathway — Continued lawful residence may support later permanent residency or citizenship eligibility, subject to law.
visa_d2.comparison_heading: D2 Entrepreneur Visa vs Golden Visa
visa_d2.comparison_d2_3: ! Usually requires genuine residence ties to Portugal
visa_d2.comparison_d2_5: ✓ Can support a long-term residence pathway
visa_d2.comparison_gv_3: ✓ Often involves lower physical presence requirements
visa_d2.comparison_gv_5: ✓ Also used by applicants seeking long-term residence options
visa_d2.comparison_bottom: In practical terms, the D2 is usually better suited to applicants who want to run an active business in Portugal, while investment-based residency routes are aimed at a different profile.
visa_d2.eligibility_intro: Each application is assessed on its own facts, but authorities generally expect clear evidence that the project is viable and the applicant is prepared.
visa_d2.eligibility_1: Business plan — A coherent plan showing market logic, implementation steps, financial assumptions, and expected activity in Portugal.
visa_d2.eligibility_2: Available capital — Evidence that the business and the applicant's relocation can be funded responsibly.
visa_d2.eligibility_3: Relevant background — Experience, qualifications, or commercial rationale supporting the proposed activity.
visa_d2.eligibility_4: Clean criminal record — Applicants are generally expected to provide acceptable criminal record documentation.
visa_d2.eligibility_5: Health insurance — Appropriate health coverage is usually required for the initial stage of the process.
visa_d2.process_1_text: We review your business concept, profile, and likely document needs to assess whether the route appears suitable.
visa_d2.process_2_text: Where relevant, this can include company setup support, tax number arrangements, banking preparation, and early business documentation.
visa_d2.process_3_text: We help structure the business narrative and supporting documentation so the application is coherent and professionally presented.
visa_d2.process_4_text: The file is assembled with supporting evidence, translations where required, and practical preparation for consular submission.
visa_d2.process_5_text: After approval, the process continues in Portugal for the residence permit stage under the relevant authority's rules and scheduling.
visa_d2.why_us_intro: We focus on practical preparation, coherence of documentation, and a structured process for business-based residency cases.
visa_d2.why_us_1: Business structuring support — Guidance on positioning the business concept and presenting its practical logic.
visa_d2.why_us_2: Document coordination — Help organising corporate, financial, personal, and supporting records.
visa_d2.why_us_3: Application preparation — Structured support for the D2 file from planning through submission readiness.
visa_d2.why_us_4: Post-approval coordination — Practical guidance for the next stage of the residence process in Portugal.
visa_d2.sectors_heading: Examples of Business Activities Commonly Considered
visa_d2.sectors_intro: Many legitimate commercial sectors may be considered under the D2 route, depending on the quality of the case and the supporting evidence.
visa_d2.sectors_tech_items: Software services, SaaS businesses, e-commerce projects, digital consulting
visa_d2.sectors_pro_items: Consultancy, design, training, specialist advisory services, agency work
visa_d2.sectors_trade_items: Import-export activity, hospitality concepts, tourism services, retail or operational businesses
visa_d2.faq_1_q: Is there a minimum investment for the D2 visa?
visa_d2.faq_1_a: There is often no single universal amount. The stronger question is whether your project appears appropriately funded for its real business needs and whether the evidence supports that position.
visa_d2.faq_2_q: Do I need to create jobs?
visa_d2.faq_2_a: Job creation can strengthen a case, but not every viable project depends on immediate hiring. The business model, credibility, and economic logic are all relevant factors.
visa_d2.faq_3_a: A wide range of lawful business activities may be considered, provided the proposal is genuine, coherent, and supported by credible evidence.
visa_d2.faq_4_q: Do I need a business plan?
visa_d2.faq_4_a: In most cases, yes. A professionally structured business plan is one of the most important elements of a persuasive D2 application.
visa_d2.faq_5_a: Family options may be available, subject to the government's rules on dependants, financial means, and supporting documents.
visa_d2.faq_6_q: How long does D2 processing take?
visa_d2.faq_6_a: Timing depends on preparation, consular scheduling, and government processing. Applicants should expect timelines to vary and avoid relying on guaranteed dates.
visa_d2.faq_7_q: Can the D2 lead to citizenship?
visa_d2.faq_7_a: Continued lawful residence may contribute toward future permanent residence or citizenship eligibility, but only under the official rules in force at that time.
visa_d2.faq_8_q: What if the business changes after approval?
visa_d2.faq_8_a: Business realities can change. The correct approach depends on the facts, the stage of residence, and the rules in force. Any material change should be assessed carefully before action is taken.
visa_d2.cta_lead: Request an eligibility review and we will assess whether your business profile appears suitable for Portugal's D2 residency pathway.

visa_d7.meta_title: Portugal D7 Visa 2026 — Passive Income Residency Support | WINIT
visa_d7.meta_description: Learn about the Portugal D7 visa for retirees and applicants with passive income. Explore income expectations, documentation, and the residency process.
visa_d7.hero_title: Portugal D7 Visa 2026 — Residency for Passive Income Applicants
visa_d7.hero_subtitle: The D7 route is commonly used by non-EU applicants with stable recurring income from sources such as pensions, dividends, rentals, royalties, or other regular passive income.
visa_d7.overview_heading: What is the D7 Passive Income Visa?
visa_d7.overview_p1: The D7 visa is a Portuguese residency route for applicants who can show regular, reliable income from outside Portugal. It is often used by retirees, investors, and other individuals whose living expenses can be supported without relying on Portuguese employment.
visa_d7.overview_p2: Retirees, applicants with pension income, landlords receiving rental income, and individuals with dividends, interest, royalties, or comparable recurring income sources.
visa_d7.income_key_text: Authorities typically expect evidence of stable income and sufficient means of support. Applicants may also be expected to show funds available in a Portuguese bank account, depending on prevailing practice.
visa_d7.income_main_text: The base level often follows the Portuguese minimum income benchmark used for residency calculations. Applicants should verify the current amount before filing.
visa_d7.income_spouse_text: Additional financial capacity is usually expected for each dependent adult included in the family structure.
visa_d7.income_child_text: Extra funds are normally required for each dependent child, and total means should cover the full household unit.
visa_d7.benefits_heading: Main Advantages of the D7 Route
visa_d7.benefit1: Passive-income residency — Suitable for applicants who do not rely on Portuguese employment.
visa_d7.benefit2: Long-term residence path — The route can support ongoing lawful residence if maintained properly.
visa_d7.benefit3: Work flexibility after residence — Subject to the applicable rules, residents may have broader rights once residence is granted.
visa_d7.benefit4: Family options — Dependants may be included or reunited later, depending on official requirements.
visa_d7.benefit5: Schengen travel — Residents may travel within the Schengen area subject to applicable conditions.
visa_d7.benefit6: Access to local systems — Residency may support access to healthcare, education, and local registration processes under the law.
visa_d7.comparison_heading: D7 Passive Income vs D8 Digital Nomad
visa_d7.comparison_bottom: As a general rule, the D7 is better aligned with passive income profiles, while the D8 is intended for applicants actively earning through remote work for foreign employers or clients.
visa_d7.eligibility_intro: A strong D7 file usually combines stable income evidence with accommodation planning, financial readiness, and compliant supporting records.
visa_d7.eligibility_1: Proof of passive income — Pension statements, dividend records, rental contracts, royalty statements, or other recurring income documentation.
visa_d7.eligibility_2: Accommodation in Portugal — A lease, deed, or other acceptable evidence of planned residence in Portugal.
visa_d7.eligibility_3: Portuguese tax number — A NIF is commonly required at an early stage of the process.
visa_d7.eligibility_4: Criminal record documentation — Official record certificates are generally required, often with legalisation and translation depending on the country.
visa_d7.eligibility_5: Health insurance — Appropriate initial coverage is typically required before local registration transitions occur.
visa_d7.process_1_text: We assess your income profile, family situation, and document structure to see whether the D7 route appears viable.
visa_d7.process_2_text: Applicants commonly need a Portuguese tax number and a local bank setup, depending on the filing approach and current practice.
visa_d7.process_3_text: We help you understand what level of accommodation evidence is usually expected for a D7 application.
visa_d7.process_4_text: The file is organised with income evidence, personal documentation, supporting records, and practical submission preparation.
visa_d7.process_5_text: Following visa approval, the process continues in Portugal for the residence permit appointment and related registration steps.
visa_d7.why_us_intro: Our role is to help you present a coherent, well-organised application with the right supporting evidence.
visa_d7.why_us_1: NIF guidance — Practical support for tax number preparation and related early-stage requirements.
visa_d7.why_us_2: Bank setup support — Assistance understanding the Portuguese banking step and how it fits into the application.
visa_d7.why_us_3: Accommodation planning — Guidance on acceptable housing evidence for the D7 pathway.
visa_d7.why_us_4: Application coordination — Structured support from planning through submission readiness and post-approval next steps.
visa_d7.who_heading: Who Commonly Uses the D7 Visa?
visa_d7.who_intro: The D7 is often used by applicants whose living costs are covered by regular income streams rather than active Portuguese employment.
visa_d7.who_retirees_text: Applicants supported by pension income from abroad.
visa_d7.who_investors_text: Applicants with dividends, interest, or other investment-related income.
visa_d7.who_landlords_text: Applicants receiving recurring rent from property held outside Portugal.
visa_d7.faq_1_q: What income counts for the D7 visa?
visa_d7.faq_1_a: Authorities generally look for recurring, provable income such as pensions, rentals, dividends, royalties, and similar passive sources. The strength of the evidence matters.
visa_d7.faq_2_q: Do I need to work in Portugal?
visa_d7.faq_2_a: No Portuguese job is required to apply under the D7 route. The route is based on financial self-support through stable income.
visa_d7.faq_3_q: Do I need money in a Portuguese bank?
visa_d7.faq_3_a: In many cases, applicants are expected or strongly advised to show available funds in Portugal. The exact expectation can vary with practice and timing.
visa_d7.faq_4_a: Family inclusion may be possible, but additional financial means and supporting documentation are usually required for each dependant.
visa_d7.faq_5_q: How long does D7 processing take?
visa_d7.faq_5_a: Timing varies according to document readiness, appointment availability, and government processing. No timeline can be guaranteed.
visa_d7.faq_6_q: Can the D7 lead to citizenship?
visa_d7.faq_6_a: Long-term lawful residence may contribute toward future permanent residency or citizenship eligibility, subject to the official rules in force at that time.
visa_d7.cta_heading: Exploring the D7 Passive Income Route?
visa_d7.cta_lead: Request an eligibility review and we will assess whether your income profile appears suitable for the Portuguese D7 pathway.

visa_d8.meta_title: Portugal D8 Visa 2026 — Digital Nomad Residency Support | WINIT
visa_d8.meta_description: Explore the Portugal D8 Digital Nomad Visa for remote employees and freelancers. Learn about income expectations, evidence of remote work, and the application process.
visa_d8.hero_title: Portugal D8 Digital Nomad Visa 2026 — Residency for Remote Workers
visa_d8.hero_subtitle: The D8 route is designed for non-EU applicants who earn income through remote work for employers or clients located outside Portugal. It is commonly used by employees, consultants, and freelancers with an established international income base.
visa_d8.overview_heading: What is the D8 Digital Nomad Visa?
visa_d8.overview_p1: The D8 visa is a residency route for applicants who can prove qualifying remote work activity and sufficient income from foreign sources. Compared with the D7, the D8 is aimed at active earned income rather than passive income.
visa_d8.overview_p2: Remote employees, contractors, freelancers, consultants, and digital professionals who want to live in Portugal while continuing to work for entities outside Portugal.
visa_d8.income_key_text: The D8 route generally requires a higher income threshold than the D7. Applicants should verify the current benchmark and prepare strong evidence showing both income level and the remote nature of their work.
visa_d8.income_main_text: Income is commonly measured against a multiple of the Portuguese minimum wage. Applicants should verify the up-to-date threshold before applying.
visa_d8.income_remote_text: This may include an employment contract, employer confirmation letter, service agreements, invoices, payment records, and bank statements showing income from outside Portugal.
visa_d8.income_other_text: Applicants commonly need accommodation evidence, a Portuguese tax number, a criminal record certificate, and appropriate health insurance for the initial stage.
visa_d8.benefits_heading: Main Advantages of the D8 Route
visa_d8.benefit1: Remote-work residency — Live in Portugal while continuing to serve foreign employers or clients.
visa_d8.benefit2: Structured long-term path — The route can support ongoing lawful residence if maintained properly.
visa_d8.benefit3: Schengen mobility — Residents may travel within the Schengen area subject to applicable rules.
visa_d8.benefit4: Family options — Dependants may be included or joined later under the official framework.
visa_d8.benefit5: Access to local systems — Residency may support healthcare, education, and other registration rights according to law.
visa_d8.benefit6: No Portuguese employer required — The route is built around foreign-source remote work income.
visa_d8.comparison_heading: D8 Digital Nomad vs D7 Passive Income
visa_d8.comparison_bottom: The D8 is normally the better fit where income comes from active remote work, while the D7 is usually better suited to passive-income profiles.
visa_d8.eligibility_intro: A strong D8 file needs both financial evidence and a clear record proving that the applicant genuinely works remotely for foreign entities.
visa_d8.eligibility_1: Evidence of remote work — Employment documentation or freelancer contracts showing ongoing work performed outside the Portuguese employment market.
visa_d8.eligibility_2: Income evidence — Payslips, bank statements, invoices, or tax records showing the required level of earned income.
visa_d8.eligibility_3: Means of support — Applicants may be expected to hold sufficient funds, including local bank evidence depending on current practice.
visa_d8.eligibility_4: Accommodation in Portugal — A lease, deed, or other acceptable housing evidence is usually required.
visa_d8.eligibility_5: NIF, criminal record, and insurance — These administrative and compliance documents are part of the standard file in most cases.
visa_d8.process_1_text: We assess your remote-work structure, income evidence, and likely suitability for the D8 route.
visa_d8.process_2_text: We guide you through early administrative steps that commonly support the application structure.
visa_d8.process_3_text: We explain what type of residence evidence is generally expected for a D8 filing.
visa_d8.process_4_text: Your remote-work, financial, identity, and supporting records are organised into a coherent application file.
visa_d8.process_5_text: After visa approval, the process continues in Portugal for the residence permit stage and associated registrations.
visa_d8.why_us_intro: We help remote professionals build a clear and credible application file for Portugal.
visa_d8.why_us_1: NIF preparation — Practical guidance for one of the key early administrative steps.
visa_d8.why_us_2: Banking support — Help understanding the local banking element and how it fits into the process.
visa_d8.why_us_3: Accommodation guidance — Support in understanding the housing evidence usually required.
visa_d8.why_us_4: Application coordination — End-to-end preparation support for remote-work residency cases.
visa_d8.who_heading: Who Commonly Uses the D8 Visa?
visa_d8.who_intro: This route is generally suitable for applicants actively earning through remote work for entities outside Portugal.
visa_d8.who_remote_text: Professionals employed by companies abroad who can work fully online.
visa_d8.who_freelance_text: Independent contractors serving foreign clients and able to document ongoing assignments and income.
visa_d8.who_digital_text: Consultants, developers, marketers, designers, and similar professionals with a portable remote-income model.
visa_d8.faq_1_q: What income is required for the D8 visa?
visa_d8.faq_1_a: The threshold is generally set above the D7 level and commonly linked to a multiple of the Portuguese minimum wage. Applicants should confirm the current benchmark before applying.
visa_d8.faq_2_q: Do I need to work for a Portuguese company?
visa_d8.faq_2_a: No. The D8 route is aimed at foreign-source remote work income rather than local Portuguese employment.
visa_d8.faq_3_q: What proof of remote work is needed?
visa_d8.faq_3_a: The exact evidence depends on your work model, but it often includes contracts, employer letters, invoices, payment records, and bank statements demonstrating remote work for foreign entities.
visa_d8.faq_4_a: Family inclusion may be possible, but additional financial capacity and supporting records are typically required.
visa_d8.faq_5_q: How long does D8 processing take?
visa_d8.faq_5_a: Timing varies according to document readiness, appointment scheduling, and government processing. It is not possible to guarantee a fixed timeline.
visa_d8.faq_6_q: Can the D8 lead to citizenship?
visa_d8.faq_6_a: Long-term lawful residence may count toward later permanent residence or citizenship eligibility, subject to the law and official requirements in force at that time.
visa_d8.cta_heading: Considering the D8 Digital Nomad Route?
visa_d8.cta_lead: Request an eligibility review and we will assess whether your remote-work profile appears suitable for Portugal's D8 pathway.
